docs.title: docs
docs.text: Descriptions of the content of each subdirectory in the FFRD standard directory structure is provided on this page.

docs.title: basin-data
docs.text:
The basin-data directory stores all official shared datasets for the project, which are delivered in the scoping and data preparation phase. A Data Manifest table will be stored in the basin-data root directory and maintain a listing of all data included in the directory along with key attributes for each dataset

Ad-hoc sharing of unofficial data shall not be posted to the basin-data directory.

docs.title: Folder includes:
docs.text:
• collected survey data
  • stream channel data extracted from 1D models
  • depth maps
  • historic topography and contour data
  • silt range surveys
  • sediment surveys
  • fish maps
  • other sources of bathymetric data

docs.title: coastal
docs.text: Folder includes information related to coastal boundaries, nodal data including simulated storm peak hydrographs and frequency data (CHS data).

docs.title: Folder includes:
docs.text:
• Information regarding dams in the study area (e.g., risk assessment data, WCMs, O&M manuals, survey data, as-built drawings, dam watch data, major reservoir’s operational guidance, NRCS data)
  • Any reporting of efforts to refine the NID data for the purposes of the FFRD study are also included here.
  • If system-response curves are generated during the data preparation phase, this information can be included within the dams folder. This information should be promoted to the later calibration or conformance phase folders once it has been reviewed and meets the criteria for these later phases.

docs.title: NID (National Inventory of Dams) Subfolder
docs.text: Folder includes static version of the National Inventory of Dams dataset collected during Data Preparation Phase. This NID data includes geospatial information.

docs.title: nid
docs.text: Folder includes static version of the National Inventory of Dams dataset collected during Data Preparation Phase. This NID data includes geospatial information.

docs.title: elevation-derived-hydrography
docs.text:
Folder includes stream hydrography developed using the full resolution base DEM, which is created during the Data Preparation Phase.

This hydrography should include attribute data for each stream segment (such as contributing drainage area above each segment). If an unattributed dataset is provided, it should be clearly named and distinguished in its own folder.

docs.title: existing-models
docs.text:
This folder contains existing H&H models (e.g. hydraulic, hydrologic, reservoir-operations) to be used as reference during FFRD model development.

These models may be sourced from the following locations (additional model sources beyond this list are likely): * Corps Water Management System (CWMS) modeling effort * USACE District Models * High-Level Risk Assessments * FEMA models * Other local and Federal agencies

All models included in this folder should include associated documentation, reporting, and metadata.

Note that non-model FEMA data and reports are saved in a separate "FEMA" directory.

docs.title: existing-reports-and-references
docs.text: This folder contains reports and documentation which is not associated with specific models or dams.

docs.title: fema
docs.text:
Folder includes static version of existing FEMA National Flood Hazard Layer including effective flood zone polygons collected during Data Preparation Phase, and FIS reports.

Note that and FEMA models are stored separately in the existing-models directory.

docs.title: flood-imagery
docs.text:
Folder includes imagery of historic flooding. This information could be from aerial sources (satellite or other aerial vehicle) or may be taken of flood conditions from the ground. This information used to aid in model development and calibration.

Flood imagery should include metadata or geospatial information providing context for the user, such as the location and date of imagery.

docs.title: government-boundaries
docs.text:
Folder includes geospatial data (such as shapefiles and raster data) of city, county, state, and other government boundaries.

U.S. Census Bureau Urban Areas polygons are to be stored in a separate folder, specifically:

basin-data > project-scoping > mesh-refinement-scope

docs.title: landcover
docs.text:
Folder includes base land cover data collected during Data Preparation Phase (NLCD, C-CAP, or similar).

Imperviousness grids are also stored in this location (MRLC)

Note this data may be modified during model development and the modified data should be stored with the relevant H&H modeling.

docs.title: levee-data
docs.text:
Folder includes information regarding levees in the study area, such as risk assessment reports, water control manuals (WCMs), survey data, as-build drawings, local and Federal information. Levee data may be geospatial or not.

Also includes Levee Data Tracking Table that is used to track data completion of refinement efforts prior to the FFRD study

docs.title: NLD (National Levee Database) Subfolder
docs.text: Folder includes static version of the National Levee Database (NLD) dataset collected during Data Preparation Phase.

docs.title: nhd
docs.text: Folder includes static version of then National Hydrography Dataset (NHD) waterway information collected during Data Preparation Phase. NHD data includes several geospatial datasets of interest, including stream centerlines and waterway types.

docs.title: observed-data
docs.text:
Compilation of centralized official of observed for project use. Sources of and availability of observed data varies by project location. This data is collected and curated during the Data Preparation Phase. This data has been adjusted to account based on QC efforts and may include:

  • flow
  • stage
  • historic dam operations data
  • gridded precipitation
  • gridded SWE
  • gridded temperature
  • Annual Maximum Series
  • Uncertainty Bounds for Annual Maximum Series
  • Stage-discharge rating curve data

Stream gage location data in a geospatial format is stored in a separate location: 

basin-data > project-scoping > calibration-metrics

docs.title: other
docs.text: Folder includes information gathered which does not readily fit into other basin-data directories.

docs.title: project-scoping
docs.text:
Folder includes key inputs that define the scope of the study, created during the Scoping and Data Preparation Phase. Refer to the FFRD Scope of Work (SOP) document for official guidance on project scoping.

Several subfolders are used to define this scoping, which include:

docs.title: name-table
docs.text: Tables that pre-define key model element names for the study that is developed during the Scoping and Data Preparation Phase.

docs.title: model-linking-register
docs.text: Model linking register for the project that clearly shows the source of all required inputs for each model. Includes a version for the calibration phase (when observed flows for historic events are commonly used as inputs) and for the conformance phase (when all inputs are based on modeled outputs).

docs.title: calibration-scope
docs.text:
Includes:

  • Geospatial file containing locations of primary, secondary, and tertiary stream gage locations

  • Calibration Event Table: Tabulation of historic events selected for calibration

docs.title: dam-scoping-table
docs.text: Inclusion for operations modeling, inclusion for fragility, and criteria for how a dam is modeled.

docs.title: uncalibrated-hydrologic-model
docs.text: Uncalibrated hydrologic model that included all planned HMS model elements including subbasins established during scoping and data preparation phase of the FFRD project.

docs.title: levee-modeling-scope
docs.text: Levee breach location geospatial file. Progresses from draft during scoping and data preparation phase to final during subsequent phases.

docs.title: hydrulic-model-units
docs.text: Polygons defining the scoped boundaries of individual hydraulic models.

docs.title: mesh-development-criteria
docs.text: Designated key streams, other streams and urban areas that define the scope for mesh development according the hydraulics section of FFRD SOP.

docs.title: study-area-polygon
docs.text: Polygon defining the study area for the project

docs.title: observed-data-linking-table
docs.text: Table of the relationship of each observed data location to its corresponding scoped model element name.

docs.title: risk-data
docs.text: The risk-data directory stores all existing official risk information for dams and levees collected prior to the FFRD study, including risk assessment reports and supporting models.

docs.title: soils
docs.text: Folder includes information on soils throughout the model area, such as gSURGGO and STATSGO data.

docs.title: storm-catalog
docs.text: Folder includes the transposition region, SST storm catalog, metadata for storm catalog (seasonality and storm typing), normalization grid and fishnets to define valid storm placement

docs.title: Sub-directories
docs.text: seasonality-distributions storms transposition-region fishnets normalization-grid

docs.title: stream-gages
docs.text:
A attributed geospatial point file of all stream gages in the basin for reference.

Note that a separate curated stream gage file is posted to the scoping-data directory, but that one includes identification of the scope for use of data at each gage in model calibration.

docs.title: structure-inventory
docs.text: Folder includes Structure Inventory data prepared during the scoping and data preparation phase. Data is prepared at the full basin level, and at the individual model unit level These datasets remain static throughout the project and are used as a reference. Any adjustments to the datasets will be captured in separate directories (within the calibration, conformance and production phases)

docs.title: TRANSPORTAION
docs.text: Folder includes information on transportation infrastructure, such as bridges, roadways, railways, and culverts.

docs.title: vertical-datum-conversions
docs.text: This directory stores a table of official vertical datums and conversion for the FFRD project. The table includes adopted conversions for Stream Gages, Dams and other points of interest.

docs.title: wbd
docs.text: Folder includes static version of USGS Watershed Boundary Datasets (WBD, i.e. HUC boundaries) collected during data preparation phase. These data may be provided at various HUC resolutions (e.g. HUC12, HUC08, etc.).

docs.title: calibration
docs.text:
This directory contains individual models through event-based calibration phase. These models and associated datasets undergo review and back check. If model files are updated due to a review, the previous version is first moved to the archive directory of individual model folders. This update and archival process may continue as models progress from draft to final delivery.

Models are promoted to the conformance phase when review is complete and the calibration phase is closed out.

Note that documentation and terrain files associated with each hydraulic model are posted to separate directories (documentation and terrain-modeling and should not be included here).

docs.title: breach-data
docs.text: Includes information required to implement breaching within the hydraulic model. Including breach locations and system response data. Information regarding breach parameters may also be included here.

docs.title: consequences
docs.text:
This folder contains the current set of structure inventories during the calibration phase, ultimately these inventories will be the ones promoted to the conformance phase.

The nsi year should reflect the year (version) of the nsi, and the year of the price level should reflect the price level year if prices are indexed.

Naming convention: <modeling-unit>-nsiyyyy-pricelevelyyyy.gpkg

docs.title: archive
docs.text:
This folder contains archived structure inventories that represent earlier versions of the inventory in the calibration phase.

The nsi year should reflect the year (version) of the nsi, and the year of the price level should reflect the price level year if prices are indexed, the yyyymmdd repreesents the date of the archive of the inventory.

Naming convention: -nsiyyyy-pricelevelyyyy-yyyymmmdd.gpkg

docs.title: hydraulics
docs.text:
Description:

This folder includes subfolders, each corresponding to an individual model-unit containing the HEC-RAS model used for the conformance phase of modeling.

Models should include all relevant files required to simulate the model on desktop. This includes all model files associated with historic events used for calibration. Any referenced DSS files (such as observed data used as boundary conditions) should be included. Observed data linked as reference, such as rating-curves, flow and stage hydrographs, should also be included in the model. Associated land-use/cover files should also be included.

Note that terrain files are not to be stored in this directory, they are located in:

ffrd-basin > terrain-modeling

docs.title: archive
docs.text:
The "archive" folder is used to house draft and outdated model versions as updates are made. Archived models should be date stamped. Examples of an archived model unit directory name:

model-unit_YYYYMMDD

kickapoo_20250314

docs.title: model-unit folder
docs.text: Includes current model results for the calibration phase.

docs.title: Description:
docs.text:
This folder contains hydrologic model resulting from the Calibration phase. This model is promoted to the Conformance folder when review is complete and the Calibration phase is closed out.

Models should include all relevant files required to simulate the model on desktop. This includes all model files associated with historic events used for calibration. Any referenced external data should be included.

docs.title: Historic-Events Folder
docs.text: The historic-events folder contains event-based hydrologic models where individual calibration/validation events were simulated.

docs.title: Continuous-Simulation Folder
docs.text: The continuous-simulation folder contains the period-of-record (POR) simulation hydrologic model.

docs.title: Archive Folder
docs.text:
The archive folder is used to house draft and outdated model versions as updates are made. Archived models should be date stamped. Examples of an archived model unit directory name:

basin-name_YYYYMMDD

trinity_20250314

docs.title: Description:
docs.text:
This folder contains reservoir-operations model resulting from the Calibration phase. This model is promoted to the Conformance folder when review is complete and the Calibration phase is closed out.

Models should include all relevant files required to simulate the model on desktop. This includes all model files associated with historic events used for calibration. Any referenced external data should be included.

The HEC-ResSim model should be placed within a subfolder of its own to ease in file management.

docs.title: Archive Folder
docs.text:
The archive folder is used to house draft and outdated model versions as updates are made. Archived models should be date stamped. Examples of an archived model unit directory name:

basin-name_YYYYMMDD

trinity_20250314

docs.title: storm-catalog
docs.text: This folder contains individual .dss files representing precipitation events which form the basis of the calibration precipitation-frequency.

docs.title: conformance
docs.text:
Model files for the conformance phase are stored in the Model Library watershed repository under conformance. Final individual models from the calibration phase are promoted to the conformance directory, integrated, executed in cloud compute, and adjusted to match long term observed data in frequency space. When adjustments are necessary to individual models, outdated models are moved to the archive subdirectory and replaced with the current version. Integrated models run through many simulations in cloud compute and results are stored here.

Note that documentation and terrain files associated with each hydraulic model are posted to separate directories (documentation and terrain-modeling and should not be included here).

docs.title: consequences
docs.text:
This folder contains the set of structure inventories during the conformance phase, ultimately these inventories will be the ones promoted to the production phase.

The nsi year should reflect the year (version) of the nsi, and the year of the price level should reflect the price level year if prices are indexed.

Naming convention: <modeling-unit>-nsiyyyy-pricelevelyyyy.gpkg

docs.title: consequences
docs.text:
This folder contains archived structure inventories that represent earlier versions of the inventory in the conformance phase.

The nsi year should reflect the year (version) of the nsi, and the year of the price level should reflect the price level year if prices are indexed, the yyyymmdd repreesents the date of the archive of the inventory.

Naming convention: -nsiyyyy-pricelevelyyyy-yyyymmmdd.gpkg

docs.title: event-validation
docs.text: This folder stores the complete model and final results of replication of each historic event through the full model suite that has been prepared for cloud compute. These model simulations act as a QC step to ensure the fully linked system of models still reproduce historic event results.

docs.title: Event Validation Runs should:
docs.text:
• Validate linked models reproduce observed calibration events sufficiently on a desktop environment
  • Prepare the model for reproduction of the calibration events in a cloud environment
  • Have 1 person responsible for these scenarios, including desktop simulation.
  • No use of observed data (other than as reference data for comparison to modeled results)
  • SST-ready naming conventions, links, etc
  • upload entire directory (all models: HMS, ResSim, RAS) after validation on desktop
  • no cloud-compute involved

docs.title: Description:
docs.text:
This folder includes subfolders, each corresponding to an individual model-unit containing the HEC-RAS model used for the conformance phase of modeling.

Models should include all relevant files required to simulate the model on desktop. This includes all model files with *.X01 extensions (e.g. .p01, .p01.hdf, .g01, .u01, etc.). Associated land-use/cover files should also be included.

Note that terrain files are not to be stored in this directory, they are located in:

ffrd-basin > terrain-modeling

docs.title: model-unit folder
docs.text: Includes current model results for the conformance phase, as improved from the calibration phase.

docs.title: archive folder
docs.text:
The "archive" folder is used to house draft and outdated model versions as updates are made. Archived models should be date stamped. Examples of an archived model unit directory name:

model-unit-YYYYMMDD kickapoo-20250314

docs.title: Description:
docs.text: This folder includes the HEC-HMS model used for the conformance phase of modeling. See Job Aid 13 - Model Delivery for Cloud Compute for certain adjustments to model files required prior to delivery.

docs.title: archive folder
docs.text:
The "archive" folder is used to house draft and outdated model versions as updates are made. Archived models should be date stamped. Examples of an archived model unit directory name:

model-unit-YYYYMMDD trinity_20250314

docs.title: manifests
docs.text: This folder contains information defining how each cloud process links to other processes. For example, it allows for a user to to reproduce all details of how data flows from seeds to consequences, and everything in between.

docs.title: Description:
docs.text: This folder includes the HEC-ResSim model used for the conformance phase of modeling.

docs.title: archive folder
docs.text:
The "archive" folder is used to house draft and outdated model versions as updates are made. Archived models should be date stamped. Examples of an archived model unit directory name:

model-unit-YYYYMMDD trinity_20250314

docs.title: EVENT-DATA
docs.text: This folder contains results from event simulations. It includes subfolders for each event simulated as part of the conformance phase. The subfolder name/number corresponds to the simulated event number. Each event contains model results for the three models (RAS, HMS, ResSim). These simulation model results are separated by the various models and include the following result data:

docs.title: hydraulics (includes subfolders for each model-unit)
docs.text:
• model-unit.p01.hdf - HEC-RAS model results from simulation, which include event-conditions, geometry, plan data, and result data.
  • model-unit.p01.tmp.hdf - HEC-RAS input data required for cloud simulation.
  • ras-event-summary.json - Summary data for many of the hydraulic model components. These include information on the 2D area (cumulative net precipitation, volume accounting error, etc.), boundary condition lines, breaching status, reference line peaks (flows, water surface elevation (WSEL), and velocities), structure variable peaks, and overall model summary data (computation time, number of cores, run time window, solution status, and information if model went unstable).
  • rasoutput.log - Runtime summary log file generated during RAS simulation. Includes information such as RAS version used for cloud simulation, simulation progress, iteration status, volume accounting error information, water surface elevation error, and other errors/warnings which occurred during the simulation.

docs.title: hydrology
docs.text:
• SST.dss - Contains timeseries (and paired) data of HMS model output.
  • exported-precip.dss - Contains gridded precipitation data in dss format. (This is an optional output file, generated by request to Cloud Compute Lead. It is required to simulate a given cloud simulated event on desktop.)
  • exported-precip.tmp.hdf - Contains gridded precipitation data in hdf format required for cloud compute.

docs.title: reservoir-operations
docs.text:
• 1h_peaks.csv - Summarized peak hourly output data from ResSim elements.
  • 24h_peaks.csv - Summarized peak 24-hour output data from ResSim elements.
  • 48h_peaks.csv - Summarized peak 48-hour output data from ResSim elements.
  • 72h_peaks.csv - Summarized peak 72-hour output data from ResSim elements.
  • ffrd.simperiod - ResSim time window which has been edited to match the HMS time window to facilitate seasonal pool operations.
  • simulation.dss - Contains timeseries (and paired) data of ResSim model output.

docs.title: system-response
docs.text: • failure_elevations.json - Sampled failure/breach elevations for each structure. Includes "location" (e.g. NID or NLD identifier) and "failure_elevation" (elevation at which a breach is triggered).

docs.title: EVENT-VALIDATION
docs.text: This folder stores the final results of replication of each historic event through the full model suite that has been prepared for cloud compute. These model simulation results act as a QC step to ensure the fully linked system of models still reproduce historic event results.

docs.title: LOGS
docs.text: This folder stores log information on HMS simulations, including simulation times and information on simulation failures.

docs.title: STORM-CATALOG
docs.text: • storms.csv - Provides an index of storm event numbers, .dss file paths, x and y coordinates (corresponding to a given "fishnet"), sampled start time (dependent on the storm's seasonaility), sample antecedent condition, and sampled calibration event parameters. This is essentially output from the SST process.

docs.title: SUMMARY DATA
docs.text:
Contains summarized data from simulated events. Summarized data are divided by model type (i.e. hydraulics, hydrology, reservoir-operations).

  • Hydraulics Summary Data - summary data is provided for individual model-units. Includes parameters such as max/min flow and WSEL data for model elements like bc-lines, reference-lines, reference-points. Overall hydraulic model information is also included, such as computation time, simulation status (e.g. finished successfully), time at which solution may have gone unstable, and volume accounting information (including cumulative net precipitation and volume accounting error).

  • Hydrology Summary Data - summary data is provided for for 1-hr, 24-hr, 48-hr, and 72-hr output periods for HMS model elements.

  • Reservoir-Operations Summary Data - summary data is provided for for 1-hr, 24-hr, 48-hr, and 72-hr output periods for ResSim model elements.

  • Plots - Contains graphical representation of simulated events. These plots include flow and stage frequency plots, stage-discharge rating curves, volume-accounting-error versus precipitation, and precipitation frequency plots. The plots provide an interactive interface to zoom and query specific events of interest based on their plotting position. Several of the plot types also display observed and/or published data for direct comparison to modeled results.

  • Additionally, breach plots are included which summarized breach data for dams and levees. Information is available for individual structures (e.g. specific levees), and also for all structures across many simulated events.

docs.title: Additional Files in the simulations folder
docs.text:
• blocks_fixed_length.json - Defines the relationship of events to synthetic years (i.e. blocks), and the relationship between blocks and realizations.
  • Combined_AMS_Contributing_10yr.csv - Defines the list of events which contribute to annual maximum series. This is also the list of events to be computed in HEC-RAS.
  • Combined_AMS_Contributing_Explain_10yr.csv - Provides a rationale for each event which contributes to the annual maximum series.

docs.title: event-validation
docs.text:
This folder stores the final results of replication of each historic event through the full model suite that has been prepared for cloud compute. These model simulations act as a QC step to ensure the fully linked system of models still reproduce historic event results.

The 'simulations/event-validation' folder stores cloud computed results of a replication of the historic events through the full H&H model suite.

docs.title: storm-catalog
docs.text:
This folder contains the storm catalog for hte basin.

The catalog.grid files which contain pointers to all storms in the catalog. It also includes the transposition domain and watershed boundary.

The storms subdirectory contains all precipitation and temperature data in .dss file format.

The fishnets folder contains the points which are valid placements for each storm.

Seasonality distributions folder are the storm typed seasonalities and govern when storms of a given type can occur within the calendar year.

docs.title: system-response
docs.text:
This folder contains tabular dam and levee system response curves which describe breach probability for individual structures and breach locations. Fragility curves are stored as comma separated value (.csv) files according to the template provided in SOP

Breach location information (geospatial and tabular) is maintained in the basin-data directory throughout the project and should not be duplicated here.

docs.title: documentation
docs.text:
The documentation directory is the centralized storage of all work plans, reports, and reviews for the entire project. The documentation will advance from draft stages to final products within this location

Documentation will be divided by both model type (e.g. hydraulics, hydrology, reservoir-operation) and also by workflow phases (e.g. calibration, conformance, production). All aspects of the project should be well documented in this directory, including components like meteorology and consequences.

docs.title: Documentation Folder Structure:
docs.text:
• Calibration spreadsheets
  • Reports
  • Review-checklists
  • Work-plans

docs.title: production
docs.text:
Model files for the production phase are stored in the Model Library watershed repository under production. Final models from the conformance phase are promoted to the production directory and production simulations are executed in cloud compute and output data is stored here. When hot fix model edits are needed for individual events, a hotfix subdirectory will be created to store the model versions.

Note that documentation and terrain files associated with each hydraulic model are posted to separate directories (documentation and terrain-modeling and should not be included here).

docs.title: consequences
docs.text:
This folder contains the set of structure inventories during the production phase, these inventories are promoted from the conformance phase.

The nsi year should reflect the year (version) of the nsi, and the year of the price level should reflect the price level year if prices are indexed.

Naming convention: <modeling-unit>-nsiyyyy-pricelevelyyyy.gpkg

docs.title: hot-fix
docs.text: The hot-fix folder contains data used to address issues identified which do not merit full-scale rerunning of all production runs. A hot fix is an alternate version of the input model(s) that addresses a specific accuracy or stability issue that arises in one or more events during the production phase. Hot fix model versions are applied only to specific events where they were deemed necessary.

docs.title: hydraulics
docs.text:
This folder includes subfolders, each corresponding to an individual model-unit containing the HEC-RAS model promoted from the conformance phase of modeling after meeting review requirements. These are the models to be used for the production phase runs.

Models should include all relevant files required to simulate the model on desktop. This includes all model files with *.X01 extensions (e.g. .p01, .p01.hdf, .g01, .u01, etc.). Associated land-use/cover files should also be included.

docs.title: HOT-FIX
docs.text: The hot-fix folder contains data used to address issues identified which do not merit full-scale rerunning of all production runs. A hot fix is an alternate version of the input model(s) that addresses a specific accuracy or stability issue that arises in one or more events during the production phase. Hot fix model versions are applied only to specific events where they were deemed necessary.

docs.title: hydrology
docs.text: This folder includes the HEC-HMS model promoted from the conformance phase of modeling. This is the model to be used for the production phase runs.

docs.title: HOT-FIX
docs.text: The hot-fix folder contains data used to address issues identified which do not merit full-scale rerunning of all production runs. A hot fix is an alternate version of the input model(s) that addresses a specific accuracy or stability issue that arises in one or more events during the production phase. Hot fix model versions are applied only to specific events where they were deemed necessary.

docs.title: manifests
docs.text: This folder contains information defining how each cloud process links to other processes. For example, it allows for a user to to reproduce all details of how data flows from seeds to consequences, and everything in between.

docs.title: reservoir-operations
docs.text: This folder includes the HEC-ResSim model promoted from the conformance phase of modeling. This is the model to be used for the production phase runs.

docs.title: hot-fix
docs.text:
The hot-fix folder contains data used to address issues identified which do not merit full-scale rerunning of all production runs. A hot fix is an alternate version of the input model(s) that addresses a specific accuracy or stability issue that arises in one or more events during the production phase. Hot fix model versions are applied only to specific events where they were deemed necessary.

test

docs.title: EVENT-DATA
docs.text: This folder contains results from event simulations. It includes subfolders for each event simulated as part of the production phase. The subfolder name/number corresponds to the simulated event number. Each event contains model results for the three models (RAS, HMS, ResSim). These simulation model results are separated by the various models and include the following result data:

docs.title: hydraulics (includes subfolders for each model-unit)
docs.text:
• model-unit.p01.hdf - HEC-RAS model results from simulation, which include event-conditions, geometry, plan data, and result data.
  • model-unit.p01.tmp.hdf - HEC-RAS input data required for cloud simulation.
  • ras-event-summary.json - Summary data for many of the hydraulic model components. These include information on the 2D area (cumulative net precipitation, volume accounting error, etc.), boundary condition lines, breaching status, reference line peaks (flows, water surface elevation (WSEL), and velocities), structure variable peaks, and overall model summary data (computation time, number of cores, run time window, solution status, and information if model went unstable).
  • rasoutput.log - Runtime summary log file generated during RAS simulation. Includes information such as RAS version used for cloud simulation, simulation progress, iteration status, volume accounting error information, water surface elevation error, and other errors/warnings which occurred during the simulation.

docs.title: hydrology
docs.text:
• SST.dss - Contains timeseries (and paired) data of HMS model output.
  • exported-precip.dss - Contains gridded precipitation data in dss format. (This is an optional output file, generated by request to Cloud Compute Lead. It is required to simulate a given cloud simulated event on desktop.)
  • exported-precip.tmp.hdf - Contains gridded precipitation data in hdf format required for cloud compute.

docs.title: reservoir-operations
docs.text:
• 1h_peaks.csv - Summarized peak hourly output data from ResSim elements.
  • 24h_peaks.csv - Summarized peak 24-hour output data from ResSim elements.
  • 48h_peaks.csv - Summarized peak 48-hour output data from ResSim elements.
  • 72h_peaks.csv - Summarized peak 72-hour output data from ResSim elements.
  • ffrd.simperiod - ResSim time window which has been edited to match the HMS time window to facilitate seasonal pool operations.
  • simulation.dss - Contains timeseries (and paired) data of ResSim model output.

docs.title: system-response
docs.text: • failure_elevations.json - Sampled failure/breach elevations for each structure. Includes "location" (e.g. NID or NLD identifier) and "failure_elevation" (elevation at which a breach is triggered).

docs.title: LOGS
docs.text: This folder stores log information on HMS simulations, including simulation times and information on simulation failures.

docs.title: STORM-CATALOG
docs.text: • storms.csv - Provides an index of storm event numbers, .dss file paths, x and y coordinates, storm type classification, and a basin path.

docs.title: SUMMARY DATA
docs.text:
Contains summarized data from simulated events. Summarized data are divided by model type (i.e. hydraulics, hydrology, reservoir-operations).

  • Hydraulics Summary Data - summary data is provided for individual model-units. Includes parameters such as max/min flow and WSEL data for model elements like bc-lines, reference-lines, reference-points. Overall hydraulic model information is also included, such as computation time, simulation status (e.g. finished successfully), time at which solution may have gone unstable, and volume accounting information (including cumulative net precipitation and volume accounting error).

  • Hydrology Summary Data - summary data is provided for for 1-hr, 24-hr, 48-hr, and 72-hr output periods for HMS model elements.

  • Reservoir-Operations Summary Data - summary data is provided for for 1-hr, 24-hr, 48-hr, and 72-hr output periods for ResSim model elements.

  • Plots - Contains graphical representation of simulated events. These plots include flow and stage frequency plots, stage-discharge rating curves, volume-accounting-error versus precipitation, and precipitation frequency plots. The plots provide an interactive interface to zoom and query specific events of interest based on their plotting position. Several of the plot types also display observed and/or published data for direct comparison to modeled results.

  • Additionally, breach plots are included which summarized breach data for dams and levees. Information is available for individual structures (e.g. specific levees), and also for all structures across many simulated events.

docs.title: Additional Files in the simulations folder
docs.text:
• blocks_fixed_length.json - Defines the relationship of events to synthetic years (i.e. blocks), and the relationship between blocks and realizations.
  • Combined_AMS_Contributing_10yr.csv - Defines the list of events which contribute to annual maximum series. This is also the list of events to be computed in HEC-RAS.
  • Combined_AMS_Contributing_Explain_10yr.csv - Provides a rationale for each event which contributes to the annual maximum series.

docs.title: system-response
docs.text:
This folder contains dam and levee system response curves which describe breach probability for individual structures and breach locations. Fragility curves are stored as comma separated value (.csv) files.

Breach location information (geospatial and tabular) is maintained in the basin-data directory throughout the project and should not be duplicated here.

docs.title: quick-share
docs.text: Temporary ad-hoc shared data can be shared among team members through the quick-share directory. This allows sharing amongst team members files that are temporary in nature, but useful for collaboration or technical discussions.

docs.title: terrain-base
docs.text: This directory contains original delivered base DEM for use in project that was gathered as part of pre-model data preparation. There should be multiple resolutions as defined in the SoP.

docs.title: terrain-mapping
docs.text:
This directory contains modified, RAS Mapper-formatted terrains used specifically in development of mapped output grids. SOP provides more information.

Terrains should generally be resampled to a single raster of a coarser resolution than used for hydraulic modeling.

docs.title: terrain-modeling
docs.text: This directory contains RAS Mapper-formatted terrains used for HEC-RAS model development. This is the terrain which can reference multiple raster DEM files and also includes vector terrain modification data. The SOP provides more information.

docs.title: This folder includes:
docs.text:
• Raster .tif DEM files
  • rasmap.hdf file which contains vector terrain modifications and other information.
  • .vrt file
